text: It’s 85° in October and my neighbors keep saying “weather.” Nah—this is climate change doing the loud part.
Renewables aren’t a fantasy: more solar + wind + storage = cleaner air and cheaper power. We can build it—if we stop stalling.
Congress loves “compromise” when it means doing less about climate. Meanwhile the planet keeps warming like it has zero patience.
Greenwashing is everywhere. If your plan doesn’t cut emissions fast, fund clean transit, and stop new fossil expansion, it’s not leadership.
“Climate is a hoax” until your roof is underwater. Reality doesn’t care about slogans.
Fossil fuel subsidies are basically a payout to pollution. Cut the funding, speed the transition, protect people first.
Bold action now: heat protections, wildfire resilience, and clean energy jobs. Waiting another election is how we lose years.
Local action matters, but so does national policy. Stop licensing new coal/oil and start investing in clean grids.
If your campaign relies on pretending climate change isn’t happening, your priorities are just… wrong.
The damage is already here—floods, fires, extreme heat. So yes, it’s a crisis. And no, “thoughts and prayers” won’t fix it.
Still getting 80° in October and my feed’s like “climate change is a hoax” 😂 Buddy, my thermometer doesn’t care about your talking points.
🚨 Congress debates again while the planet burns: one side talks jobs, the other side blocks clean energy. Which one do you think will keep us employed?
🌍 When drought hits, it’s not just “weather.” It’s food prices, water rights, and whole communities forced to move. Climate action is emergency action.
“Investing in fossil fuels” isn’t a plan—it’s postponing the bill. Clean energy is the only way to cut emissions and lower costs long-term.
My city keeps breaking heat records and people still argue on the internet. Like… go outside? The air is literally changing.
⚖️ New regulations for emissions vs. “freedom to pollute” 🙃 Spoiler: our lungs aren’t up for negotiation.
Every time leaders delay climate policy, extreme weather gets a bigger budget. Prevention is cheaper than recovery—stop pretending otherwise.
🌱 We need more renewables, faster permitting, and serious grid upgrades. Clean power shouldn’t be the hardest thing to build.
If your climate take ignores science and lives purely on vibes, you’re not “skeptical”—you’re just choosing ignorance.
Just saw another article about federal lands opened for more drilling. Meanwhile communities are flooding. Priorities are showing, and it’s ugly.
It’s wild how the forecast is like “record highs again” and then someone says climate change is fake. Nature doesn’t negotiate. 🌍🔥 Do something—vote, push policy, cut emissions.
Rant: more coal leasing + more delays = slower clean-energy rollout. You can’t “boost industry” on a dying planet. We need renewables, not backtracking. ⚡️
Electric buses + better transit = fewer emissions and cleaner air. It’s not complicated: invest where people live, breathe, and commute. 🚍🌱
“Just deal with it” is not a climate plan. Heat, storms, and smoke are here now. We need bold action—starting yesterday. 💬
Lowkey tired of the “energy needs” excuse. Clean energy can meet demand and still protect communities from pollution. Why pretend we have to choose? 🌎
Congress talks about budgets, but the atmosphere doesn’t care about excuses. Act like the crisis is real: stronger standards, faster clean energy, less waste. 🤦‍♂️
If you’re profiting from fossil fuels, you don’t get to lecture anyone about “hoaxes.” The data’s not a vibe. It’s physics. 📉🌡️
When leaders dismiss climate change, it’s families who pay: higher bills, worse air, flooded homes. Accountability matters. Demand action now. 🏠💨
Renewables + storage are getting cheaper every year—meanwhile fossil fuels keep getting subsidized like it’s the 1800s. Time to update the energy strategy. ⚡️
Hot take: pretending climate change isn’t happening is choosing the disaster. Clean energy jobs, resilience, and real solutions—let’s go. 💚
It’s October and I’m sweating in a hoodie. Then someone says “climate change isn’t real” 🙃 Like… open your eyes. This is what warming looks like.
🔥 Hot take: the “record warmth” isn’t a weather fluke. It’s climate change doing what it does—again. Stop gaslighting people with vibes. Demand clean energy + real accountability. 💚
🌍 If your city keeps setting heat records, that’s not “normal.” That’s climate impacts. More funding for cooling centers, resilience, and emissions cuts—now. 🧊
🚨 Congress wants more delays on climate action while emissions keep climbing. Meanwhile families pay the price with higher bills and worse air. Fix this. Prioritize people. Not profits.
So tired of the “it’s just weather” crowd—like extreme storms and wildfire smoke are supposed to be a fun seasonal activity 🙃 Climate change is here. Ask your lungs.
⚡ Renewable energy isn’t a pipe dream. It’s jobs, cheaper power, and less pollution. We can argue policy, but we can’t deny physics anymore.
There’s a reason people don’t trust politicians on climate: they talk big, then approve fossil fuel giveaways. Transparency, enforcement, and actual targets—or stop pretending.
🌱 Protecting forests = protecting the climate. Every summer of fires makes that clearer. Fund reforestation, responsible land management, and clean energy—together, not in pieces.
“Why should I care?” Because your commute, your healthcare, and your groceries get hit first. Climate change is economic change. Act like it matters. 📉🌡️
⚫️ When leaders cut regulations for polluters, the harm shows up everywhere: schools, highways, coastal towns. Climate justice is just justice. Period.
Cold front? Sure. Still doesn’t erase the bigger trend. Stop cherry-picking single days—climate change is measured in decades, not memes. 📊
It’s November and it’s still insanely warm like… this isn’t “weather,” it’s climate change showing up in real time. Your “it’s just a season” excuses are getting old. 🌍🔥
BREAKING: more wildfires, more heat, more floods—then politicians act shocked. We keep funding the problem and calling it “policy.” Climate action isn’t optional anymore. 💬
Climate change isn’t abstract when your power bill spikes, your air is smoky, and summers last forever. Protect people first—clean energy now. ⚡️🌱
Hot take: “wait and see” is just another way of saying “let people suffer now.” We already know what emissions do. The only question is how fast we act. ⏳
Renewables aren’t a vibe—they’re cheaper, cleaner, and job-creating. Stop subsidizing pollution and start building the future. That’s it. ✅💚
Congress loves deregulation until the disaster hits. Then suddenly everyone wants “solutions.” Start with cutting greenhouse gases—no delay. 🚨
My favorite thing is watching officials deny climate reality while climate disasters pile up on the evening news. Must be nice living in denial. 🙃
Electric buses, rail, heat pumps—these are real tools, not talking points. Fund the upgrades and let emissions drop. Simple. 🚆⚡️
If you’re still calling it a hoax, check the records. Then check the science. Then check the smoke in your lungs. Truth isn’t optional. 😤🌫️
The planet doesn’t negotiate. You can’t lobby your way out of physics. Cut fossil fuels, expand clean power, and protect communities—today. 🌎✊
It’s not “just weather” when it keeps happening year after year. Heat waves, floods, fires—climate change is here. We deserve action, not excuses. 🌎🔥
🚨Congressional hearings are a mess again: lobbyists want delay, communities need clean air and real protections. Stop protecting profits, start protecting people. 💬⚖️
Renewables aren’t a fantasy—they’re already cheaper and faster to build. The only “green scam” is delaying clean energy while emissions climb. ⚡🌱
If your power bill goes up and the climate gets wilder, that’s not a mystery. Invest in efficiency + clean energy now. Before “too late” becomes normal. 🏠💡
Hot take: calling climate change a hoax while you climate-control your mansion is wild. Reality doesn’t care about your denial. 🥴🌡️
More extreme weather = more strain on schools, hospitals, and local budgets. Climate action is public health policy. Period. 🩺🌧️
We keep funding coal leases like it’s 1990. Meanwhile the planet keeps running the numbers. Clean energy isn’t optional—it’s urgent. 🌍⏳
Electric buses, safer transit, better infrastructure—those are climate solutions *and* quality-of-life improvements. Let’s build it. 🚍💚
It’s exhausting seeing politicians treat greenhouse gases like they’re negotiable. Science isn’t a debate club. Act like you believe it. 📉🌬️
Hotter summers and longer wildfire seasons aren’t inevitable—they’re consequences. Choose mitigation + resilience, or keep paying later. 🔥🏞️
80° in October + “climate change is fake”?? Make it make sense. We’re watching the weather shift in real time, while people act like science is optional 🙃🌍
Reminder: renewable energy isn’t just “nice”—it’s jobs, cheaper power, and real cuts to pollution. Let’s stop treating clean energy like a debate. 💚⚡
Congress loves big talk about the economy—until it’s time to regulate emissions. Can we prioritize health and futures for once? 🤔🚨
Feds opening more fossil fuel leases while CO2 keeps climbing is peak backwards. Clean energy now, not later. 🌱📈
The climate crisis doesn’t care about politics. Heat, fires, floods—same story, different season. We need action that matches reality. 🌪️🔥
If your solution to climate change is “wait and see,” congrats—you’re gambling with everyone else’s lives. 🎲🌍
New study: most people want stronger climate action. Meanwhile only 2% of giving goes toward protecting people and the planet. What are we doing?
Climate change is here—and so are the costs. Build cleaner infrastructure, upgrade the grid, and electrify transportation. That’s the plan. ⚙️🚗
People chanting “hoax” usually can’t explain how emissions magically disappear. Science isn’t a mood. 🤷‍♂️🌡️
Private jets and yachts calling climate change a scam is wild. Funny how the pollution is “fake” until it hits your backyard. ✈️🛥️
It’s October and my AC is still fighting for its life. Meanwhile politicians keep acting like climate change is a “debate.” 🌡️ We’re literally watching it happen.
Hot take: “economic growth” that depends on burning the planet is a scam. Invest in renewables + efficiency—cheaper, cleaner, and safer. 💚
Congress says energy independence, then blocks climate action. Make it make sense. 🤷‍♂️ Clean grids, transit, and real enforcement—stop protecting polluters.
🚨Remember when they said emissions cuts were impossible? Now the tech is here—heat pumps, EVs, wind/solar. The only thing missing is political will.
If you still think climate change is fake, tell that to the wildfire smoke in your lungs and the flood photos on your feed. 🌪️🌫️
Another warm “seasonal” surprise. My body’s confused, my bill’s rising, and billionaires tell us not to worry. Cool. 🙃
We can argue budgets later. Right now, we need policies that cut emissions and protect frontline communities—no half-measures, no delay.
Renewable energy isn’t utopia—it’s jobs, innovation, and lower bills. Stop letting fossil lobbyists slow the inevitable. ⚡️
When leaders deny science, communities pay. More storms, more heat, more loss. Let’s stop pretending prevention is optional.
Fossil fuel profits keep soaring while carbon pollution wrecks summers, springs, and safety. It’s time for bold clean energy—period. 🌍
It’s wild: October is basically summer now, and folks still argue about climate change 🙃 Hotter days, bigger storms—reality doesn’t care about your opinion.
BREAKING: new push to keep fossil fuels rolling while the planet heats up again and again. Follow the money, then tell me it’s not a scam.
Renewables aren’t “nice to have” anymore—they’re the most practical way to cut pollution and save money. Let’s fund clean energy like it’s urgent (because it is).
80 degrees in October and my AC bill is like 😭 Climate change is real, visible, and expensive.
Congress wants debate, the atmosphere wants action. Invest in clean power, public transit, and resilient infrastructure—before disasters get worse.
Climate change is here—wildfires, floods, heat waves. Stop pretending it’s political and start treating it like the emergency it is.
Honestly tired of hearing “wait and see” while greenhouse gases keep climbing. We’re already paying the price—let’s stop escalating it.
If you’re worried about jobs, look at clean energy hiring. Cutting emissions can mean more work, not less.
Stop opening more land for coal and drilling. Clean energy is cheaper, safer, and we can’t afford another decade of pollution.
Reminder: one bad election cycle won’t undo CO2. Climate solutions need long-term commitment, not slogans.
